user.title: Connect to Jellyfin
user.description:
Enter the details of your Jellyfin server to get started.

**Finding your API token:** In Jellyfin, go to **Dashboard → API Keys** and create a new key with the **+** button.

**Poll interval:** How frequently Home Assistant checks for active sessions. Lower values are more responsive but increase server load.

data_description.host: IP address or hostname of your Jellyfin server
data_description.token: Generate an API key in Jellyfin → Dashboard → API Keys

select_users.title: Select Users
select_users.description:
{plugin_status}

Choose which Jellyfin users to monitor. Each selected user will get a dedicated sensor in Home Assistant showing their current playback status.

reconfigure.title: Update Server Connection
reconfigure.description: Update the connection details for your Jellyfin server.

data_description.host: IP address or hostname of your Jellyfin server
data_description.token: Generate an API key in Jellyfin → Dashboard → API Keys

reconfigure_users.title: Update Monitored Users
reconfigure_users.description:
{plugin_status}

Update which Jellyfin users to monitor.

error.cannot_connect: Unable to connect to Jellyfin. Check that the host and port are correct and the server is reachable.
error.invalid_auth: Invalid API token. Generate a new one in Jellyfin under Dashboard → API Keys.
error.no_users_selected: At least one user must be selected.
error.unknown: An unexpected error occurred. Check the Home Assistant logs for details.

abort.already_configured: JellyMon is already set up. Use the Reconfigure option to change settings.
